tasks.title: Trace durable chat recovery
tasks.prompt: Trace how a persisted AI conversation is rebound and recovered after editor restart. Identify the durable identities, the reconstruction entry point, and each condition that refuses unsafe recovery. Do not edit files.
success_criteria: Names the reconstruction entry point and durable identity types
Distinguishes recoverable queued work from ambiguous in-flight work
Cites paths and symbols for every lifecycle claim

tasks.title: Explain scheduler invariants
tasks.prompt: Explain the provider-independent responsibilities and invariants of AgentDispatchScheduler, including causal parent linkage, writer exclusion, and lifecycle validation. Do not propose implementation changes.
success_criteria: Separates durable scheduling from provider execution
Explains parent turn/event causality
Explains both shared-writer and read-only workspace checks

tasks.title: Map durable run storage
tasks.prompt: Map the run storage layout from root discovery through encoded IDs, manifests, artifacts, and SQLite metadata. Report traversal and permissions defenses. Do not edit files.
success_criteria: Reports root precedence and ID encoding
Explains artifact versus metadata storage
Identifies owner-only directory behavior and traversal defenses

tasks.title: Trace AI context construction
tasks.prompt: Trace how Ovim constructs bounded AI context from editor state and project context. Identify where selections, diagnostics, symbols, and project instructions enter, and where bounds or redaction apply.
success_criteria: Identifies the context data structures and construction path
Accounts for project instructions and editor-derived facts
Reports concrete size or redaction boundaries

tasks.title: Explain Codex session binding
tasks.prompt: Explain how Codex app-server threads and turns are started, resumed, and bound to Ovim durable identities. Highlight how provider IDs are kept distinct from Ovim IDs.
success_criteria: Names provider and Ovim identity types
Traces start and resume request construction
Identifies validation that prevents a mismatched resume

tasks.title: Map tool registration and policy
tasks.prompt: Map the path from an AI tool definition to schema exposure, scope policy, execution, and result presentation. Identify duplicated sources of truth or drift risks without changing code.
success_criteria: Traces definition through execution
Identifies capability or scope enforcement points
Supports any drift claim with at least two concrete symbols

tasks.title: Trace an AI file mutation
tasks.prompt: Trace one AI file-edit tool from provider tool call through path validation, expected-revision checking, buffer mutation, save behavior, event recording, and user-visible result. Report gaps explicitly.
success_criteria: Covers schema, policy, mutation, and presentation boundaries
Identifies the optimistic concurrency check
Distinguishes buffer state from disk state

tasks.title: Audit prompt precedence
tasks.prompt: Audit edit and chat system-prompt precedence from configuration through provider request construction. State which overrides are interpolated and how project context is appended.
success_criteria: Gives the exact edit precedence order
Gives the exact chat precedence order
Distinguishes interpolated and raw prompt sources

tasks.title: Trace selected code into AI requests
tasks.prompt: Trace selected editor text into AI edit and chat requests, including coordinate conversion, language/file metadata, and any snapshot or revision used to prevent stale application.
success_criteria: Identifies selection extraction and request types
Accounts for path and language metadata
Explains the stale-edit defense or states its absence precisely

tasks.title: Trace file provenance through replay
tasks.prompt: Trace how base manifests, file mutation events, and artifacts reconstruct a historical workspace. Explain divergence detection and how branch-local changes remain distinct.
success_criteria: Explains base and transition artifacts
Explains divergence checks
Accounts for created, deleted, and branch-local files

tasks.title: Trace headless AI chat lifecycle
tasks.prompt: Trace a headless AI chat turn from API or command input through the editor event loop, background provider polling, snapshot updates, and durable completion. Identify where TUI and headless paths converge.
success_criteria: Names the transport-to-editor request path
Identifies the shared background polling path
Explains when durable turn completion is recorded

tasks.title: Explain the Linux AI tool test fix
tasks.prompt: Using the current tests and recent commit history, explain why AI tool tests differed on Linux, what invariant the fix restored, and which nearby cases could regress. Do not edit files.
success_criteria: Identifies the platform-specific cause from code or history
Names the affected invariant and tests
Proposes focused regression checks without inventing failures

tasks.title: Triage scheduler recovery failures
tasks.prompt: Assume the scheduler recovery tests report that a running shared writer remains Running after rehydrate and a second writer is accepted. Locate the likely faulty transition or lease reconstruction and give a minimal verification plan.
success_criteria: Identifies the recovery ambiguity rule
Connects terminal interruption to lease release
Proposes tests that distinguish projection failure from scheduler failure

tasks.title: Triage a run-log causality rejection
tasks.prompt: Assume a test fails with an invalid-history error saying an agent lifecycle is not causally contiguous after restart. Trace how caused_by is validated and list the smallest set of corrupted or missing events that can produce the error.
success_criteria: Locates the precise validation
Distinguishes missing, reordered, and cross-agent causes
Gives a deterministic reproduction strategy

tasks.title: Triage Codex resume mismatch
tasks.prompt: Assume resuming a durable Codex chat returns a different native thread ID than the stored binding. Explain how Ovim should classify the failure, what must remain durable, and what it must not silently do.
success_criteria: Identifies the thread-ID validation path
Preserves Ovim identity and durable trace
Rejects unsafe silent rebinding and explains recovery state

tasks.title: Review AI tool path safety
tasks.prompt: Review AI tool path handling from security, correctness, and user-experience perspectives. Prioritize concrete findings involving traversal, symlinks, excluded paths, unsaved buffers, or error clarity.
success_criteria: Separates security findings from usability observations
Prioritizes findings by plausible impact
Cites exact code and avoids speculative vulnerabilities

tasks.title: Review run-log durability
tasks.prompt: Review run-log persistence from crash consistency, schema evolution, replay fidelity, and operability perspectives. Report confirmed strengths as well as actionable risks.
success_criteria: Covers all four requested perspectives
Distinguishes confirmed behavior from inference
Provides evidence for each high-priority risk

tasks.title: Review dispatch concurrency
tasks.prompt: Review current dispatch scheduling for race safety, durable causality, restart behavior, and future supervisor integration. Do not implement the supervisor.
success_criteria: Evaluates shared writer exclusion and lifecycle edges
Evaluates process-restart ambiguity
Identifies integration seams without pretending live execution exists

tasks.title: Review AI state consistency
tasks.prompt: Review whether AI chat state is represented consistently across the core editor, terminal renderer, and headless API. Focus on status, pending attention, errors, and durable versus transient state.
success_criteria: Traces at least one state field end to end
Identifies any divergent presentation with evidence
Separates durable source state from UI-only projection

tasks.title: Review tool schema-policy drift
tasks.prompt: Review the AI tool system for ways schema, provider exposure, approval policy, dispatch, and result formatting can drift. Recommend a smallest coherent boundary, but do not refactor it.
success_criteria: Finds concrete duplicated tool knowledge
Explains an observable failure mode for drift
Proposes a bounded direction consistent with existing registry patterns

tasks.title: Document one prompt precedence chain
tasks.prompt: Read resolve_edit_system_prompt and return its precedence chain in exact order with one sentence about interpolation. Do not inspect unrelated files and do not edit anything.
success_criteria: Lists all precedence levels in source order
Correctly identifies which fallback is raw

tasks.title: Check one lifecycle edge
tasks.prompt: Determine whether Queued may transition directly to Completed in AgentDispatchScheduler and cite the function that decides it. Return only the answer and evidence.
success_criteria: Answers no
Cites valid_transition and the relevant edge

tasks.title: Report run-root precedence
tasks.prompt: Report the run storage root precedence implemented by RunStorageLayout in at most three bullets. Do not edit files.
success_criteria: Names the environment override
Names the platform-data fallback and ovim/runs suffix

tasks.title: Find the context-reset event name
tasks.prompt: Find the value of CONVERSATION_CONTEXT_RESET_EVENT and return its defining path. Nothing else.
success_criteria: Returns conversation_context_reset
Returns the defining source path

tasks.title: Find the AI HTTP timeout
tasks.prompt: Find the HTTP timeout configured by request_ai_edit and answer with the duration and defining function. Do not broaden the search.
success_criteria: Returns 120 seconds
Names request_ai_edit

tasks.title: State whether TUI sessions are automatic
tasks.prompt: Using CLAUDE.md, answer whether ordinary TUI mode automatically registers a session and give the opt-in command. Use two sentences at most.
success_criteria: States that TUI session registration is opt-in
Gives :session start NAME
